task:
Fix ticket 0040: the daemon silently launches a stale built Oz dashboard bundle. Implement a built-bundle staleness check in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts and add a discoverable one-command UI rebuild. Choose the REFUSE-WITH-MESSAGE design (do NOT auto-rebuild inside the launch request).

WHAT TO CHANGE:

1) packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts — in resolveDashboardLaunch (around line 1273): when BOTH built entries (out/main/main.js and out/renderer/index.html) exist, before returning the built plan, compute whether the bundle is STALE relative to packages/ui source. Definition of stale: the newest mtime among packages/ui source files is NEWER than the bundle build time. Bundle build time = the OLDER (min) of the two built entries' mtimes (out/main/main.js and out/renderer/index.html). Source scan = recursive walk of packages/ui EXCLUDING the directories 'out', 'node_modules', and any dotfile/cache dirs ('.turbo', '.vite', etc.); take the max mtime of files found. If the newest source mtime > bundle build time => STALE.
   - Fresh bundle (build time >= newest source) => return the built plan unchanged (mode 'built'), exactly as today.
   - Stale bundle => return { ok: false, error: <clear message> }. The message MUST say the built bundle is stale relative to packages/ui source and name the ONE command to run to rebuild: `pnpm build:ui`. Do not fall back to dev mode on stale (that would silently change behavior); refuse explicitly so the request surfaces 409.
   - Missing/partial built entries => unchanged existing behavior (fall through to dev script, else the existing 'no launchable entry' 409).
   Keep helpers small and pure (e.g. a newestSourceMtime(dir) walker and an isStale comparison). Do not change packageManager/hasDevScript/isFile contracts. Do not change dev-mode resolution at all.

2) root package.json — add a 'build:ui' script: "build:ui": "pnpm --filter @cocoder/ui build". Keep existing scripts intact and ordering sensible.

ACCEPTANCE / TESTS (extend packages/daemon/tests/dashboard-launch.test.ts):
- The makeHome helper currently writes built files without controlling mtimes. Add the ability to make the bundle FRESH (built entries' mtime newer than source) and STALE (a packages/ui source file mtime newer than built entries). Use fs utimes / explicit mtime control, or write source after built with a forced newer mtime — be deterministic, do not rely on wall-clock ordering races.
- New test: fresh built bundle => result mode 'built', spawns the built command (existing 'prefers the built dashboard entry' test must still pass; make its bundle fresh).
- New test: stale built bundle (a source file newer than the bundle) => status 409, error contains 'stale' and 'pnpm build:ui', and NOTHING is spawned (fixture.spawns is empty).
- Existing tests for dev-only, partial-built fallback, missing-entry 409, double-launch, and spawn-failure must all still pass.

MUST NOT BREAK / CONSTRAINTS:
- No change to dev-mode behavior or the built-vs-dev resolution contract beyond inserting the staleness gate on the built branch.
- Do not auto-run a build inside resolveDashboardLaunch or requestDashboardLaunch (no spawning builds in the launch path).
- Do not weaken any existing assertion to make new code pass.

VERIFY EVIDENCE TO PRODUCE: run `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` (or the daemon vitest) and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck` (or root typecheck) and report exit codes + the dashboard-launch test results. Show the actual diff of launcher.ts, package.json, and the test file.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts, packages/daemon/tests/dashboard-launch.test.ts, package.json. Do not touch packages/ui source or any other file.
